title: Economics in the Global Age
description: This exercise will cover the continuities and changes of the global economy since 1900 (ECN) as well as how global social systems have been challenged and maintained (SIO). Each theme will have a model and will scale 5 items, for a total of 12.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following claims best supports the argument that globalization has had some positive effects?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The global playing field has become “flatter,” or more level.
Food shortages around the globe have been completely eradicated.
Contagious diseases have decreased in prevalence and severity.
Biodiversity and environmental conservation have increased.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following pieces of evidence would best support a historian’s argument that globalization has caused inequalities?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The economic output of New York City alone is the same size as Brazil’s.
Many Western European nations expanded social welfare systems in the mid-20th century.
Post-Soviet states experienced hyperinflation and unemployment.
Many post-colonial economies in Latin America and Africa are dependent on single commodity exports.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best identifies a major limitation of Mao Zedong’s Great Leap Forward in China?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: unrealistic targets, drought, and poor management
bad weather, natural disasters, and construction of infrastructure
economic reforms, the introduction of market elements, and privatization
political purges, attacks on intellectuals, and the rejection of traditional belief systems

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>What is there to be afraid of? So long as we keep this attitude, everything will be all right, and we shall not make any major mistakes. In short, if we want socialism to achieve superiority over capitalism, we should not hesitate to draw on the achievements of all cultures and to learn from other countries, including the developed capitalist countries, all advanced methods of operation and techniques of management that reflect the laws governing modern socialized production.

Source: Deng Xiaoping, speech on the difference between socialism and capitalism, January 18 to February 21, 1992, China. 

**Based on the excerpt above and your knowledge of world history, in which of the following ways was Deng Xiaoping’s leadership in China similar to that of Mao Zedong in the latter half of the 20th century?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They both wanted to increase production and modernize China.
They both rejected industrial knowledge from capitalist countries.
They both favored collectivized farming.
They both promoted centralized planning to industrialize.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>All along the border, the rule of law has become hollow in the face of the desire by authorities to avoid doing anything to discourage US investors, including enforcing Mexican law. It is not simply a Mexican problem, however. As President [George W.] Bush pushes to extend NAFTA across two continents, and Congress seems ready to give him fast track authority to negotiate the Free Trade Area of the Americas, the [problems at a maquiladora] may be duplicated over and over again…
>
>As usual, what appears to be a legal problem is really about money.
>
>Last spring, Torreon’s streets filled with women chanting and shouting demands for a return to a standard of living capable of providing something better than cardboard houses and communities without sewers, electricity and running water. The city’s annual May Day parade witnessed over 2000 women shouting, “we won’t be quiet anymore!” and “we want a decent life!”

Source: David Bacon, “The Fruits of NAFTA” 2002, Mexico. 

**The excerpt above illustrates a continuation of which of the following developments in Latin America which resulted from globalization from 1900 to the present?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The exploitation of local labor and resources by foreign investors, leading to poor living conditions and economic inequality.
The struggle for women’s rights and increased political activism in response to gender inequality.
The increasing integration of Latin American economies into global markets, resulting in higher GDPs and economic growth.
The rise of leftist populist leaders advocating wealth redistribution in response to inequalities caused by free trade agreements.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>[The five member states] HEREBY DECLARE:
>
>FIRST, the establishment of an Association for Regional Cooperation among the countries of South-East Asia to be known as the Association of South-East Asian Nations (ASEAN).
>
>SECOND, that the aims and purposes of the Association shall be:
>
>To accelerate the economic growth, social progress and cultural development in the region through joint [endeavors] in the spirit of equality and partnership in order to strengthen the foundation for a prosperous and peaceful community of South-East Asian Nations;
>
>To promote regional peace and stability through abiding respect for justice and the rule of law in the relationship among countries of the region and adherence to the principles of the United Nations Charter;
To promote active collaboration and mutual assistance on matters of common interest in the economic, social, cultural, technical, scientific and administrative fields;

Source: The ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration), 1967, Bangkok, Thailand. 

**A historian could use the excerpt above as evidence of which of the following developments in the time period of 1900 to the present?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rise of regional organizations to foster economic growth and political stability after the end of imperialism.
The formation of international trade agreements designed to enhance global commerce and reduce tariffs among industrialized nations.
The creation of a globalized economic system that maintained colonial power structures and wealth disparities.
The exploitation of workers in factories in Asia by multinational corporations as a result of global industrialization.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Protests against the World Trade Organization in the 1990s reflect which of the following broader political trends in the late 20th century?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of anti-globalization movements
the resurgence of communism
the decline of trade unions
the increasing attention to child labor issues

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.** 

>SEC. 601. No person in the United States shall, on the ground of race, color, or national origin, be excluded from participation in, be denied the benefits of, or be subjected to discrimination under any program or activity receiving Federal financial assistance.

Source: Civil Rights Act, The United States of America, 1964. Archives. 

**Which of the following best summarizes how the legislation above was achieved in the United States in the 1960s?** 


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: lawsuits, boycotts, and peaceful rallies
militarism, revolts, and charismatic leadership
tax resistance, occupying government offices, and mass hunger strikes
insurrection, economic withdrawal, and strikes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.** 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Movements like the one reflected in the image above best demonstrate a response to which of the following consequences of globalization?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Photo of indigenous Latin American people marching down a street holding flags and signs.
options.caption: Source: Members of the Confederation of Indigenous Nationalities of Ecuador (CONAIE) march in Quito, Ecuador against the 2002 summit of the Free Trade Area of the Americas. 31 October 2002. Creative Commons.

choices.content: It endangered indigenous cultures and the environment.
It led to mass unemployment in developing nations.
It supplied unequal economic opportunities to men and women.
It made economies in Latin America more dependent on export-driven industries.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best identifies an impact of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Activists challenged racist apartheid practices.
Future genocides were prevented by international action.
Global standards for human rights made all nations equal.
Human rights violators were prosecuted and punished.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Since the United States has been a major force in globalization, American ideas about human rights have spread worldwide. Which of the following best illustrates the limitations of globalization in advancing human rights?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Workers at a Nike factory in Vietnam work in dangerous conditions with no protections.
Textile factories in Bangladesh face increasing pressure to eliminate sweatshops.
Women in India have had more access to job opportunities.
Labor organizers at a Ford factory in Guatemala were given lower-paying contracts than those in Detroit.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following could a historian use as evidence of the positive impacts of nationalism in the twentieth century?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: In Rwanda, nationalism promoted a unified identity and helped to encourage reconciliation following a genocide.
In Italy during the 1930s, new roads and railways modernized the infrastructure.
In the United States, civic actions like sit-ins and marches led to laws that promoted racial equality.
In India, religious differences were put aside to build the Indian government after it won independence from Great Britain.
